overview.title: Scanner Matrix & Platform Skills
overview.subtitle: Scanners handle collection, Skills encapsulate reusable platform capabilities — together covering comments, negative reviews, Q&A, search suggestions, and local communities.
overview.description: 13 platform scanners cover Reddit, YouTube, Google, Amazon, Quora, Twitter/X, V2EX, Weibo, and local platforms. Each scanner has a corresponding Skill that encapsulates input/output/throttling/link policy.

highlights.label: 13 Platforms
highlights.desc: From Reddit to local platforms — covering English and Chinese communities

highlights.label: 12 Skills
highlights.desc: Each Skill encapsulates input/output/rate-limit/Link Policy for composable orchestration

highlights.label: Tiered Scheduling
highlights.desc: Scanning tasks distributed by platform Tier — cost and value matched automatically

scanners.title: 13-Scanner Matrix
scanners.subtitle: Each platform uses a dedicated scanning method with differentiated frequency, rate-limiting, and targeting strategy.

scanners.focus: Negative posts and help-seeking discussions inside subreddits
Complaints and follow-up questions in tutorial video comments
People Also Ask, autocomplete, and related search suggestions
1–3 star reviews and Verified Purchase feedback
Real-time complaints, venting, and strong-emotion signals

scanners.rateLimit: Collect reviews from the last 90 days
Do not re-scrape the same app within 14 days
scanners.focus: Android-side negative reviews and feature complaints
Pros/Cons and reviewer job titles from Trustpilot, G2, and Capterra
Ask HN and lightly discussed unresolved technical threads
Technical questions with no accepted answer or zero answers
Product/tech discussion signals from Chinese developer community
Product complaint signals from trending topics and keyword search

skills.title: 10 Platform Skills
skills.subtitle: Each Skill encapsulates complete input parameters, output structure, and publishing policy — enabling automated scanning-to-reply orchestration.

skills.output: Comment list + frustration markers + Channel ID
skills.linkPolicy: Prefer hyperlinked text; if needed, route into a DM follow-up queue
Check subreddit rules first; if external links are restricted, use product name + DM follow-up

skills.output: Questions with high follow count and few answers
skills.linkPolicy: Max one external link per answer; if collapsed, fall back to product name or resource page
URLs allowed; inject tracked t.co links directly

skills.output: Reviews, pros/cons, and reviewer job titles
skills.linkPolicy: Read-only signals used for pain-point validation

skills.output: Low-score or lightly discussed technical threads
skills.linkPolicy: For highly relevant posts, cite a GitHub bridge only after a complete technical answer

skills.output: Questions with zero answers or no accepted answer
skills.linkPolicy: A complete solution must come first, followed by a commercial hosted-option link
